one_line_ask: Denoise and de-hiss six raw sleep-lesson narration takes into clean voiceover masters, then inspect them to produce a transcript/chapter pack, and hand everything back as one organized, zipped course-audio package.
brief:
Stillwell is a small sleep-and-mindfulness education platform. We are building a self-paced course module titled 'Easing Insomnia: A Six-Lesson Wind-Down', and we recorded the narration ourselves at home. The problem is the audio: our host recorded the six lessons on a USB mic in a home office, so every take has steady room hiss, a faint HVAC hum, a bit of laptop-fan whine, and uneven levels where the voice drifts loud and soft. The script is finished and the delivery is warm and calm, which we love — we just need the VOICE cleaned up so it is clear, consistent, and soothing enough for people to fall asleep to without a hiss buzzing in their ears.

What we need: (A) take the six raw lesson narration takes (lesson_01 through lesson_06, WAV) and clean each one so the room hiss, hum and fan noise are gone and the host's voice sits clear and balanced — this is voice cleanup only (denoise / de-hiss), NOT music mixing or a full master; we will lay the soft sleep-music bed under it and do the final fades and loudness pass ourselves on our side. (B) Because these become the course-player lessons, we also need a transcript-and-chapter pack: a short content summary of each cleaned lesson so
